sessions.intent: Design DataSource persistence layer and Settings variable bridge for Runtime2

before.assumptions: No DataSource exists in Runtime2 yet. Spec files provided via mounted folder at /shared/architect/system_datasource/. Settings system exists but has no persistence layer. MemoryStack resolves %var% synchronously.
before.risk: Async/sync bridge between DataSource and MemoryStack variable resolution. Resolved: SQLite has no async I/O, so .GetAwaiter().GetResult() is safe.

sessions.plan: See v1/plan.md — DataSource interface + SQLite impl, SettingsData with virtual GetChild for per-key lazy loading, AskError on missing keys, LazyParamsGenerator error propagation fix.

actions.context: Architectural plan for DataSource + Settings bridge. Key design decisions: per-key loading (not bulk), virtual GetChild on Data, SettingsData owns navigation, error propagation in LazyParamsGenerator.

sessions.intent: Implement DataSource persistence layer, SettingsData bridge, AskError, and LazyParamsGenerator error propagation

before.assumptions: Architect plan v1 is approved. Microsoft.Data.Sqlite already in PLang.csproj. No existing DataSource directory. GetChild on Data is not virtual yet. LazyParamsGenerator does not propagate resolution errors.
before.risk: SQLite sync-over-async bridge in SettingsData.GetChild. GetChild becoming virtual may affect existing Data subclasses (DynamicData, Data<T>). LazyParamsGenerator change affects all action handlers.

sessions.plan: Create IDataSource interface + SqliteDataSource implementation, DataSourceError/AskError types, SettingsData with virtual GetChild override for per-key lazy loading. Modify Actor.cs (lazy DataSource property, SettingsData registration on System actor). Modify LazyParamsGenerator for error propagation via __resolutionError. Create settings action handlers (get/set/remove). Write C# tests for DataSource and SettingsData.

actions.context: DataSource interface and SQLite implementation. SettingsData extends Data with virtual GetChild for per-key lazy loading from SQLite.
Error types for DataSource operations and missing required values.
Settings action handlers following existing handler pattern (partial class, [Action] attribute, IContext, Run()).
Made GetChild virtual so SettingsData can override it.
Added lazy DataSource property (.db/{actorname}.sqlite), registered SettingsData on System actor's MemoryStack, dispose DataSource.
Added __resolutionError field. Modified __Resolve<T> to use MemoryStack.Get() for full-match variables and check for errors. Added error check before Run().
23 C# tests (13 DataSource + 10 Settings). All 1446 total tests pass.

after.health: All 1446 C# tests pass. All 15 passing PLang tests still pass. 8 pre-existing PLang failures unrelated to this change.
after.notes: Data.GetChild is now virtual - this is a small API surface change that could affect any code overriding or mocking Data. No existing subclasses were affected. LazyParamsGenerator change affects all generated action handlers but is backward-compatible (new field defaults to null, existing behavior unchanged when no errors).

sessions.intent: 5-pass code analysis of DataSource + Settings Bridge implementation

before.assumptions: Implementation is complete per coder session. All existing tests pass. Need to verify OBP compliance, simplification opportunities, readability, behavioral correctness, and test coverage gaps.
before.risk: May find untested code paths or OBP violations that need addressing before merge.

sessions.plan: Read all 14 changed files. Perform 5-pass analysis (OBP Compliance, Simplification, Readability, Behavioral Reasoning, Deletion Test). Write detailed findings to result.md and verdict to verdict.json.

actions.context: 5-pass code analysis. Found: good OBP compliance, untested error propagation pipeline, untested SanitizeTableName, MemoryStack.Clone type-loss, bare catch in DeserializeValue.
Analysis output files: detailed result, verdict (fail), version summary, and cross-session summary.

after.health: Analysis complete. Verdict: FAIL. Key issues: untested error propagation pipeline, untested SQL injection defense, MemoryStack.Clone type-loss concern, bare catch in DeserializeValue.
after.notes: OBP compliance is good across all files. The implementation is well-structured but has test coverage gaps on critical paths.
